finding_results.evidence: Six reworked sites using distinct syntactic strategies: (1) chapters/chapter_17.md:91 — Group B demonstrative 'the one that said I'm deciding whether to sit down or leave' replaced with participial 'deciding whether to sit down or leave' (clause restructuring). (2) chapters/chapter_17.md:123 — Group A double 'the way' comparison 'descriptive, the way she described data in presentations, the way she laid out what she'd observed and let the numbers do the work' replaced with direct description 'clinical, laying out what she'd observed and letting the numbers do the work.' (3) chapters/chapter_17.md:147 — Group A self-comparison 'He took it in the way he took everything in — through his body first, through his hands on the oak and his bare feet on the linoleum, through the particular feel of the kitchen air against his arms' replaced with direct physical catalogue 'He took it in through his body first — his hands on the oak, his bare feet on the linoleum, the kitchen air against his arms.' (4) chapters/chapter_17.md:161 — Unflagged Group B instance 'the building with the cracked cornice, the one Theo drew' folded into direct relative clause 'the building with the cracked cornice that Theo drew.' (5) chapters/chapter_17.md:213 — Group C double sound-as-metaphor 'the small sound of glass touching stainless steel was the sound of the conversation ending' replaced with concrete noun predication 'the clink of glass on steel ended the conversation.' (6) chapters/chapter_17.md:241 — Group B double demonstrative 'Dan's pillow, the one she hadn't sent to the couch with him, the one that sat there every night' restructured as coordinated clauses 'Dan's pillow — she hadn't sent it to the couch with him, and it sat there every night.' Post-revision counts: Group A 'the way [clause]' = 3 (lines 7, 195, 231); Group B 'the one [relative clause]' = 3 (lines 13, 21, 209); Group C sound/silence constructions = 3 (lines 53, 237, 245). All groups at or below the 3-instance ceiling.
finding_results.notes: All six reworked instances use distinct strategies (direct description, participial restructuring, clause folding, concrete noun predication, coordinated clause restructuring) rather than substituting one template for another. Line 245 ('the sound a marriage makes as it finally finishes falling') preserved as the strongest Group C instance per rewrite direction. One unflagged Group B instance at line 161 was also reworked to ensure the chapter-wide count meets the acceptance test.

summary: Single finding (f17_02, MEDIUM) addressed: three recurring syntactic templates — 'the way [clause]' comparisons, 'the one [relative clause]' demonstratives, and sound/silence-as-metaphor constructions — each reduced from 4-5 instances to 3 through targeted reworking at six sites. Each revision uses a distinct syntactic strategy (direct description, participial phrase, clause folding, concrete noun predication, coordinated clauses) to break the template repetition without homogenizing the prose. Heading contract preserved. No sanitization, no dialogue register changes, no continuity disruption.
